steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word 'However' marks a pivot: the bird's specialization first appears beneficial (aids survival in its habitat) and then becomes a liability (makes it vulnerable to environmental change). The main idea must capture both sides of this trade-off.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — habitat sharing: contradicts the passage, which says nothing about sharing habitat. ✗
  • Choice B — specialization only: ignores the vulnerability side of the trade-off entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — highly specialized and therefore particularly susceptible to habitat disturbances: 'therefore' makes the causal link explicit — vulnerability flows directly from specialization. Captures both sides. ✓
  • Choice D — inverts causality: implies disturbances cause specialization, reversing the passage's logic. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
